name: Tech Stack Selector
description: Helps teams choose the right technology stack for a new project — frontend, backend, database, and infrastructure based on requirements and team expertise

defaultWeights.label: Learning Curve
defaultWeights.description: Team ramp-up tolerance — high accepts steep learning, low picks familiar tech

defaultWeights.label: Licensing Cost
defaultWeights.description: Budget for tools and licenses — high allows paid solutions, low prefers open source

defaultWeights.label: Ecosystem Maturity
defaultWeights.description: Preference for mature ecosystems — high picks established, low tries emerging tech

defaultWeights.label: Developer Experience
defaultWeights.description: Weight on DX and tooling quality — high prioritizes great DX, low accepts rough edges

defaultWeights.label: Build Speed
defaultWeights.description: Development velocity priority — high picks fast-to-build stacks, low optimizes runtime

defaultWeights.label: Community Support
defaultWeights.description: Importance of community and docs — high needs strong community, low accepts niche tech

defaultWeights.label: Type Safety
defaultWeights.description: Static typing preference — high demands strong types, low accepts dynamic languages

defaultWeights.label: Performance Focus
defaultWeights.description: Runtime performance requirements — high needs low latency, low accepts interpreted languages

nodes.label: Project Type
nodes.description: What kind of project are you building?
nodes.prompt: What type of project are you building?

nodes.label: Expected Scale
nodes.prompt: What scale do you anticipate at peak?

nodes.label: Team Expertise
nodes.description: What does the team know best?
nodes.prompt: What is the team's strongest area of expertise?

nodes.label: Data Characteristics
nodes.description: What kind of data does the app primarily work with?

nodes.label: Cloud-Native
nodes.description: Assumes cloud-native deployment from the start
nodes.assumption: The project will be deployed to a cloud platform (Azure, AWS, or GCP) from day one. Local/on-prem deployment is not a primary requirement.

nodes.label: Frontend Framework
nodes.condition: Which frontend approach fits your project type and team?

nodes.label: Backend Framework
nodes.condition: Which backend stack aligns with your expertise and requirements?

nodes.label: Database Selection
nodes.description: Choose the right database for your data model
nodes.condition: Which database technology fits your data model and scale?

nodes.label: React + Next.js Stack
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a senior frontend architect. Provide opinionated, production-ready recommendations. Prefer convention over configuration. Include specific version numbers.

nodes.label: React + Vite SPA
nodes.template:
Generate a React + Vite SPA project scaffold.

Project: {{projectType}}
Scale: {{expectedScale}}
Team: {{teamExpertise}}

Include:
1. Vite project structure with TypeScript
2. React Router v7 setup
3. State management (Zustand for client state, TanStack Query for server state)
4. Component library recommendation
5. API client setup with interceptors
6. Build optimization (code splitting, lazy loading)
7. Docker container for deployment
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a React specialist. Provide a lean, fast SPA setup optimized for developer experience and build performance.

nodes.label: Server-Rendered Stack
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a .NET architect. Provide a practical, maintainable server-rendered setup that leverages the .NET ecosystem strengths.

nodes.label: Relational DB Setup
nodes.template:
Generate a relational database architecture recommendation.

Project: {{projectType}}
Scale: {{expectedScale}}
Data: {{dataModel}}
Cloud: {{cloudNative}}

Include:
1. PostgreSQL vs SQL Server decision matrix for this use case
2. Schema design best practices
3. Indexing strategy for common query patterns
4. Connection pooling configuration
5. Migration tool setup (EF Core Migrations / Flyway / golang-migrate)
6. Read replica strategy if needed
7. Backup and point-in-time recovery setup
8. Azure managed service recommendation (Azure SQL / Azure PostgreSQL Flexible)
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a database architect. Provide specific schema advice, not just generic recommendations. Include query patterns and index strategies appropriate to the project type.

nodes.label: NoSQL DB Setup
nodes.template:
Generate a NoSQL database architecture recommendation.

Project: {{projectType}}
Scale: {{expectedScale}}
Data: {{dataModel}}
Cloud: {{cloudNative}}

Include:
1. Cosmos DB vs MongoDB Atlas decision for this use case
2. Partition key strategy (critical for performance)
3. Document schema design with denormalization patterns
4. Consistency level selection and trade-offs
5. Request Unit (RU) estimation and autoscale configuration
6. Change Feed / Change Streams for event-driven patterns
7. Cost optimization (TTL, server-side caching)
8. SDK code sample for CRUD operations
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a NoSQL architect specializing in Azure Cosmos DB. Partition key selection is the #1 performance decision — provide specific guidance based on access patterns.

nodes.label: Node.js Backend
nodes.template:
Generate a Node.js backend API scaffold.

Project: {{projectType}}
Scale: {{expectedScale}}
Team: {{teamExpertise}}
Data: {{dataModel}}

Include:
1. Express vs Fastify recommendation for this scale
2. TypeScript project structure
3. Middleware stack (auth, validation, error handling, logging)
4. ORM recommendation (Prisma / Drizzle / TypeORM)
5. API documentation (OpenAPI / Swagger)
6. Testing setup (Vitest + Supertest)
7. Docker multi-stage build
8. Azure Container Apps deployment config
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a Node.js backend architect. Provide a type-safe, well-structured API setup. Prefer Fastify for performance at scale.

nodes.label: .NET Backend
nodes.template:
Generate an ASP.NET Core Web API scaffold.

Project: {{projectType}}
Scale: {{expectedScale}}
Team: {{teamExpertise}}
Data: {{dataModel}}

Include:
1. Minimal API vs Controller-based recommendation
2. Clean Architecture / Vertical Slice project structure
3. Authentication (MSAL / JWT bearer)
4. Entity Framework Core setup with migrations
5. MediatR + FluentValidation pipeline
6. Health checks and OpenTelemetry
7. Aspire integration for local dev
8. Azure App Service / Container Apps deployment
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a .NET architect. Provide a modern ASP.NET Core 9 setup using minimal APIs where appropriate. Follow Microsoft best practices.

nodes.label: Python Backend
nodes.template:
Generate a Python backend API scaffold.

Project: {{projectType}}
Scale: {{expectedScale}}
Team: {{teamExpertise}}
Data: {{dataModel}}

Include:
1. FastAPI vs Django recommendation for this use case
2. Project structure with src layout
3. Pydantic models for validation
4. SQLAlchemy / Tortoise ORM setup
5. Async support and connection pooling
6. Testing with pytest + httpx
7. Dockerized with uvicorn/gunicorn
8. Azure Container Apps deployment
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a Python backend engineer. Provide type-safe, async-first API setups. Use FastAPI for new projects unless Django's batteries are specifically needed.
